survivors.justification: rejects-non-numeric scenario; the assertion is type-only (TypeError). A non-numeric operand mutated to another non-numeric value still raises TypeError, so the exact value is not outcome-determining.
same scenario; a is validated first and b is also non-numeric, so the outcome is TypeError regardless of b's value.
zero-divisor scenario (b=0); the dividend is irrelevant to ZeroDivisionError, so mutating it cannot change the outcome.

killed.note: the divisor IS outcome-determining: away from 0 there is no ZeroDivisionError, the test fails, the mutant is killed. Proves the gate still catches load-bearing cells (a vacuous/hardcoded handler would let this survive => killable => BOUNCE).
